item.dc:creator: LadyNetrex

item.title: I have a bit of the Avatar stuff saved up. Today is a good day for it.
item.description:
Ah ha! My cake day has caught *me* by surprise! Fortunately i had this available for upload. I was at exactly enough images and then I say one was a 40k image. Oh well. 49 ain't bad!
I like the Avatar series. I rewatched both recently and enjoy them immensely. It's ok to like things just because you like them. Tou don't have to explain or justify it. Be a need, read tour books, paint just for the sake of painting. Your hobbies and little joys don't need anyone's approval. Anyway. Kchow!
item.dc:creator: Icouri

item.title: Kanu signs a 1 day contract with the Windsor Spitfires!
item.description: 4 decades after he was to try out for the OHL team, but was derailed by an injury, he signs a 1 day contract. He used the time to sign autographs on various items to be auctioned off to raise funds for the Canadian Mental Health Association.

item.title: It's time for Funny Friday! A humor dump to end the week
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another Funny Friday, where we try to end the work week on a high note. As always, there's a link to some raunchier comedic bonus content in the comment, so make sure not to miss it. Hope you have a good time and at least a few chuckles along the way.
15 castings of magic missile using no mana whatsoever.
Dick move Sam. God tier trolling, but dick move.
Tony Hawk the skater? I wonder what he's up to these days...
How do they do it?!
... yes. Yes they are.
Bro tried walking up playing The Rains of Castamere and everything but failed.
Scottish twitter strikes again.
You had a bad day. You're taking one down. You sing a sad song just to turn it around...
The math isn't mathing!
Talk about a photobomb...
Hope this gave you a few laughs, and you have a great weekend. Don't forget the bonus content in the comments.
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer

item.title: Nothing says manly man like a cute bright red tank top
item.dc:creator: MyNameGifOreilly

item.title: My Wolverine Cosplay
item.description:
SnarkyJayCosplay here— sharing my latest cosplay! It was meant to be simple, but I put about 28 hours into it and I’m really proud of it. I ordered this suit “custom-made,” except it wasn’t, at all. So I had to “tailor” the vest completely (I can’t sew, so it’s all hot glue and staples). The claws are plastic, ordered off Amazon but then chopped up to fit my hands better and assembled into custom-painted gloves, where I also patterned and made the little “ports” for them in silver EVA foam. Then I made the helmet from EVA foam and I weathered the whole suit with acrylic paint!
I’ve been obsessed with Wolverine for years (there’s me at age 13 with my plushie, it’s a miracle I didn’t have forks between my knuckles) so it’s really cool to finally cosplay him in an outfit I LOVE.
item.dc:creator: rectalpossum

item.title: Can relate... This is very moving
item.description: My Hira ( now in doggy heaven ) started having issues with his eyes in his elder years and lost most of his sight eventually despite eye surgery and treatment. Watching these two I can relate ...how special this moment must be!

item.title: It appears that if cops break down your door using a bad warrant and kill you, it's your fault
item.description: A Federal judge ruled that Breonna Taylor's boyfriend is the cause of her death. He opened fire on what he thought was someone breaking into the apartment, but was actually police. The police returned fire, killing Breonna Taylor. The police had made a false statement to a judge to obtain this warrant, they only had basic rumors and no solid evidence. They knew that what they had would not get them the warrant, so they lied to the judge to get him to sign a no knock search warrant. I'm not a lawyer, but this feels wrong on every level. Front page edit: Don't forget about this and write your local and federal officials about what you think of this!
item.dc:creator: qhogan11337

item.title: Tim Curry Returns to the Big Screen in Horror Movie ‘Stream’
item.description: The veteran actor will return to the big screen as a character in the horror film “Stream,” which is opening Wednesday in select theaters. It will be his first feature film role since the 2010 comedy “Burke & Hare,” even though he has worked on many animated projects as a voice actor since then. “This has been a really hard secret to keep and contain our excitement, especially for this long,” Leavy said in a statement. “We are so happy and thrilled to bring Tim back to the movies and back to the big screen! His charm always radiates and shines through everything he does and we are so honored to have him a part of ‘Stream’! He is someone I have idolized personally growing up and it is one of the major highlights of my life getting to work with him, especially on a project that means so much to me. We can’t wait for fans to sit back and enjoy our movie in theaters and hopefully with a huge crowd having a blast together! This is the way horror films are meant to be seen and enjoyed. ‘Stream’ is here to entertain!” After a stroke in 2012, Curry has focused on voice acting performances. https://variety.com/2024/film/news/tim-curry-new-horror-movie-stream-1236111766/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin
